summary: The Iran war widened sharply over the weekend as Houthi militants entered the conflict and fired missiles at Israel, sending Brent surging above $116 a barrel at the open. Simultaneously, stagflation fears are deepening: PCE inflation forecasts for 2026 have been revised up to 3.1% while consumer sentiment fell to a three-month low of 53.3, pushing real rates and bond duration risk further into stressed territory across all asset classes.
tweet: Brent spiked above $116 as Houthis joined the Iran war — PCE inflation forecasts revised up to 3.1% for 2026 — stagflation fear is now the dominant market regime.
